steps.message:
# Prometheus PromQL Query Parsing and Validation Pipeline

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in prometheus/prometheus that implement the PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline. Identify: the Parser interface and parser struct with ParseExpr and checkAST (in promql/parser/), the Lexer tokenizer, the AST node type definitions (Expr, VectorSelector, BinaryExpr, etc.), the generated yacc parser, the Function registry defining valid PromQL functions, the value type system used for expression type validation, and the Engine that integrates the parser.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: prometheus/prometheus.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `prometheus/prometheus` (prometheus/prometheus)
- `sourcegraph-testing/prometheus-common` (prometheus/client_golang)
- `prometheus/alertmanager` (prometheus/alertmanager)
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all the Go source files that implement the PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline in the prometheus/prometheus repository. Let me start by exploring the structure.

1→// Copyright The Prometheus Authors
     2→// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     3→// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     4→// You may obtain a copy of the License at
     5→//
     6→// http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     7→//
     8→// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
     9→// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    10→// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    11→// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    12→// limitations under the License.
    13→
    14→package parser
    15→
    16→import (
    17→	"fmt"
    18→	"strings"
    19→	"unicode"
    20→	"unicode/utf8"
    21→
    22→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser/posrange"
    23→)
    24→
    25→// Item represents a token or text string returned from the scanner.
    26→type Item struct {
    27→	Typ ItemType     // The type of this Item.
    28→	Pos posrange.Pos // The starting position, in bytes, of this Item in the input string.
    29→	Val string       // The value of this Item.
    30→}
    31→
    32→// String returns a descriptive string for the Item.
    33→func (i Item) String() string {
    34→	switch {
    35→	case i.Typ == EOF:
    36→		return "EOF"
    37→	case i.Typ == ERROR:
    38→		return i.Val
    39→	case i.Typ == IDENTIFIER || i.Typ == METRIC_IDENTIFIER:
    40→		return fmt.Sprintf("%q", i.Val)
    41→	case i.Typ.IsKeyword():
    42→		return fmt.Sprintf("<%s>", i.Val)
    43→	case i.Typ.IsOperator():
    44→		return fmt.Sprintf("<op:%s>", i.Val)
    45→	case i.Typ.IsAggregator():
    46→		return fmt.Sprintf("<aggr:%s>", i.Val)
    47→	case len(i.Val) > 10:
    48→		return fmt.Sprintf("%.10q...", i.Val)
    49→	}
    50→	return fmt.Sprintf("%q", i.Val)
    51→}
    52→
    53→// Pretty returns the prettified form of an item.
    54→// This is same as the item's stringified format.
    55→func (i Item) Pretty(int) string { return i.String() }
    56→
    57→// IsOperator returns true if the Item corresponds to a arithmetic or set operator.
    58→// Returns false otherwise.
    59→func (i ItemType) IsOperator() bool { return i > operatorsStart && i < operatorsEnd }
    60→
    61→// IsAggregator returns true if the Item belongs to the aggregator functions.
    62→// Returns false otherwise.
    63→func (i ItemType) IsAggregator() bool { return i > aggregatorsStart && i < aggregatorsEnd }
    64→
    65→// IsAggregatorWithParam returns true if the Item is an aggregator that takes a parameter.
    66→// Returns false otherwise.
    67→func (i ItemType) IsAggregatorWithParam() bool {
    68→	return i == TOPK || i == BOTTOMK || i == COUNT_VALUES || i == QUANTILE || i == LIMITK || i == LIMIT_RATIO
    69→}
    70→
    71→// IsExperimentalAggregator defines the experimental aggregation functions that are controlled
    72→// with EnableExperimentalFunctions.
    73→func (i ItemType) IsExperimentalAggregator() bool {
    74→	return i == LIMITK || i == LIMIT_RATIO
    75→}
    76→
    77→// IsKeyword returns true if the Item corresponds to a keyword.
    78→// Returns false otherwise.
    79→func (i ItemType) IsKeyword() bool { return i > keywordsStart && i < keywordsEnd }
    80→
    81→// IsComparisonOperator returns true if the Item corresponds to a comparison operator.
    82→// Returns false otherwise.
    83→func (i ItemType) IsComparisonOperator() bool {
    84→	switch i {
    85→	case EQLC, NEQ, LTE, LSS, GTE, GTR:
    86→		return true
    87→	default:
    88→		return false
    89→	}
    90→}
    91→
    92→// IsSetOperator returns whether the Item corresponds to a set operator.
    93→func (i ItemType) IsSetOperator() bool {
    94→	switch i {
    95→	case LAND, LOR, LUNLESS:
    96→		return true
    97→	}
    98→	return false
    99→}
   100→
   101→type ItemType int
   102→
   103→// This is a list of all keywords in PromQL.
   104→// When changing this list, make sure to also change
   105→// the maybe_label grammar rule in the generated parser
   106→// to avoid misinterpretation of labels as keywords.
   107→var key = map[string]ItemType{
   108→	// Operators.
   109→	"and":    LAND,
   110→	"or":     LOR,
   111→	"unless": LUNLESS,
   112→	"atan2":  ATAN2,
   113→
   114→	// Aggregators.
   115→	"sum":          SUM,
   116→	"avg":          AVG,
   117→	"count":        COUNT,
   118→	"min":          MIN,
   119→	"max":          MAX,
   120→	"group":        GROUP,
   121→	"stddev":       STDDEV,
   122→	"stdvar":       STDVAR,
   123→	"topk":         TOPK,
   124→	"bottomk":      BOTTOMK,
   125→	"count_values": COUNT_VALUES,
   126→	"quantile":     QUANTILE,
   127→	"limitk":       LIMITK,
   128→	"limit_ratio":  LIMIT_RATIO,
   129→
   130→	// Keywords.
   131→	"offset":      OFFSET,
   132→	"smoothed":    SMOOTHED,
   133→	"anchored":    ANCHORED,
   134→	"by":          BY,
   135→	"without":     WITHOUT,
   136→	"on":          ON,
   137→	"ignoring":    IGNORING,
   138→	"group_left":  GROUP_LEFT,
   139→	"group_right": GROUP_RIGHT,
   140→	"fill":        FILL,
   141→	"fill_left":   FILL_LEFT,
   142→	"fill_right":  FILL_RIGHT,
   143→	"bool":        BOOL,
   144→
   145→	// Preprocessors.
   146→	"start": START,
   147→	"end":   END,
   148→	"step":  STEP,
   149→	"range": RANGE,
   150→}
   151→
   152→var histogramDesc = map[string]ItemType{
   153→	"sum":                SUM_DESC,
   154→	"count":              COUNT_DESC,
   155→	"schema":             SCHEMA_DESC,
   156→	"offset":             OFFSET_DESC,
   157→	"n_offset":           NEGATIVE_OFFSET_DESC,
   158→	"buckets":            BUCKETS_DESC,
   159→	"n_buckets":          NEGATIVE_BUCKETS_DESC,
   160→	"z_bucket":           ZERO_BUCKET_DESC,
   161→	"z_bucket_w":         ZERO_BUCKET_WIDTH_DESC,
   162→	"custom_values":      CUSTOM_VALUES_DESC,
   163→	"counter_reset_hint": COUNTER_RESET_HINT_DESC,
   164→}
   165→
   166→var counterResetHints = map[string]ItemType{
   167→	"unknown":   UNKNOWN_COUNTER_RESET,
   168→	"reset":     COUNTER_RESET,
   169→	"not_reset": NOT_COUNTER_RESET,
   170→	"gauge":     GAUGE_TYPE,
   171→}
   172→
   173→// ItemTypeStr is the default string representations for common Items. It does not
   174→// imply that those are the only character sequences that can be lexed to such an Item.
   175→var ItemTypeStr = map[ItemType]string{
   176→	OPEN_HIST:     "{{",
   177→	CLOSE_HIST:    "}}",
   178→	LEFT_PAREN:    "(",
   179→	RIGHT_PAREN:   ")",
   180→	LEFT_BRACE:    "{",
   181→	RIGHT_BRACE:   "}",
   182→	LEFT_BRACKET:  "[",
   183→	RIGHT_BRACKET: "]",
   184→	COMMA:         ",",
   185→	EQL:           "=",
   186→	COLON:         ":",
   187→	SEMICOLON:     ";",
   188→	BLANK:         "_",
   189→	TIMES:         "x",
   190→	SPACE:         "<space>",
   191→
   192→	SUB:        "-",
   193→	ADD:        "+",
   194→	MUL:        "*",
   195→	MOD:        "%",
   196→	DIV:        "/",
   197→	EQLC:       "==",
   198→	NEQ:        "!=",
   199→	LTE:        "<=",
   200→	LSS:        "<",
   201→	GTE:        ">=",
   202→	GTR:        ">",
   203→	TRIM_UPPER: "</",
   204→	TRIM_LOWER: ">/",
   205→	EQL_REGEX:  "=~",
   206→	NEQ_REGEX:  "!~",
   207→	POW:        "^",
   208→	AT:         "@",
   209→}
   210→
   211→func init() {
   212→	// Add keywords to Item type strings.
   213→	for s, ty := range key {
   214→		ItemTypeStr[ty] = s
   215→	}
   216→	// Special numbers.
   217→	key["inf"] = NUMBER
   218→	key["nan"] = NUMBER
   219→}
   220→
   221→func (i ItemType) String() string {
   222→	if s, ok := ItemTypeStr[i]; ok {
   223→		return s
   224→	}
   225→	return fmt.Sprintf("<Item %d>", i)
   226→}
   227→
   228→func (i Item) desc() string {
   229→	if _, ok := ItemTypeStr[i.Typ]; ok {
   230→		return i.String()
   231→	}
   232→	if i.Typ == EOF {
   233→		return i.Typ.desc()
   234→	}
   235→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", i.Typ.desc(), i)
   236→}
   237→
   238→func (i ItemType) desc() string {
   239→	switch i {
   240→	case ERROR:
   241→		return "error"
   242→	case EOF:
   243→		return "end of input"
   244→	case COMMENT:
   245→		return "comment"
   246→	case IDENTIFIER:
   247→		return "identifier"
   248→	case METRIC_IDENTIFIER:
   249→		return "metric identifier"
   250→	case STRING:
   251→		return "string"
   252→	case NUMBER:
   253→		return "number"
   254→	case DURATION:
   255→		return "duration"
   256→	}
   257→	return fmt.Sprintf("%q", i)
   258→}
   259→
   260→const eof = -1
   261→
   262→// stateFn represents the state of the scanner as a function that returns the next state.
   263→type stateFn func(*Lexer) stateFn
   264→
   265→type histogramState int
   266→
   267→const (
   268→	histogramStateNone histogramState = iota
   269→	histogramStateOpen
   270→	histogramStateMul
   271→	histogramStateAdd
   272→	histogramStateSub
   273→)
   274→
   275→// Lexer holds the state of the scanner.
   276→type Lexer struct {
   277→	input       string       // The string being scanned.
   278→	state       stateFn      // The next lexing function to enter.
   279→	pos         posrange.Pos // Current position in the input.
   280→	start       posrange.Pos // Start position of this Item.
   281→	width       posrange.Pos // Width of last rune read from input.
   282→	lastPos     posrange.Pos // Position of most recent Item returned by NextItem.
   283→	itemp       *Item        // Pointer to where the next scanned item should be placed.
   284→	scannedItem bool         // Set to true every time an item is scanned.
   285→
   286→	parenDepth  int  // Nesting depth of ( ) exprs.
   287→	braceOpen   bool // Whether a { is opened.
   288→	bracketOpen bool // Whether a [ is opened.
   289→	gotColon    bool // Whether we got a ':' after [ was opened.
   290→	gotDuration bool // Whether we got a duration after [ was opened.
   291→	stringOpen  rune // Quote rune of the string currently being read.
   292→
   293→	// series description variables for internal PromQL testing framework as well as in promtool rules unit tests.
   294→	// see https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/unit_testing_rules/#series
   295→	seriesDesc     bool           // Whether we are lexing a series description.
   296→	histogramState histogramState // Determines whether or not inside of a histogram description.
   297→}
   298→
   299→// next returns the next rune in the input.
   300→func (l *Lexer) next() rune {
   301→	if int(l.pos) >= len(l.input) {
   302→		l.width = 0
   303→		return eof
   304→	}
   305→	r, w := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(l.input[l.pos:])
   306→	l.width = posrange.Pos(w)
   307→	l.pos += l.width
   308→	return r
   309→}
   310→
   311→// peek returns but does not consume the next rune in the input.
   312→func (l *Lexer) peek() rune {
   313→	r := l.next()
   314→	l.backup()
   315→	return r
   316→}
   317→
   318→// backup steps back one rune. Can only be called once per call of next.
   319→func (l *Lexer) backup() {
   320→	l.pos -= l.width
   321→}
   322→
   323→// emit passes an Item back to the client.
   324→func (l *Lexer) emit(t ItemType) {
   325→	*l.itemp = Item{t, l.start, l.input[l.start:l.pos]}
   326→	l.start = l.pos
   327→	l.scannedItem = true
   328→}
   329→
   330→// ignore skips over the pending input before this point.
   331→func (l *Lexer) ignore() {
   332→	l.start = l.pos
   333→}
   334→
   335→// accept consumes the next rune if it's from the valid set.
   336→func (l *Lexer) accept(valid string) bool {
   337→	if strings.ContainsRune(valid, l.next()) {
   338→		return true
   339→	}
   340→	l.backup()
   341→	return false
   342→}
   343→
   344→// is peeks and returns true if the next rune is contained in the provided string.
   345→func (l *Lexer) is(valid string) bool {
   346→	return strings.ContainsRune(valid, l.peek())
   347→}
   348→
   349→// acceptRun consumes a run of runes from the valid set.
   350→func (l *Lexer) acceptRun(valid string) {
   351→	for strings.ContainsRune(valid, l.next()) {
   352→		// Consume.
   353→	}
   354→	l.backup()
   355→}
   356→
   357→// errorf returns an error token and terminates the scan by passing
   358→// back a nil pointer that will be the next state, terminating l.NextItem.
   359→func (l *Lexer) errorf(format string, args ...any) stateFn {
   360→	*l.itemp = Item{ERROR, l.start, fmt.Sprintf(format, args...)}
   361→	l.scannedItem = true
   362→
   363→	return nil
   364→}
   365→
   366→// NextItem writes the next item to the provided address.
   367→func (l *Lexer) NextItem(itemp *Item) {
   368→	l.scannedItem = false
   369→	l.itemp = itemp
   370→
   371→	if l.state != nil {
   372→		for !l.scannedItem {
   373→			l.state = l.state(l)
   374→		}
   375→	} else {
   376→		l.emit(EOF)
   377→	}
   378→
   379→	l.lastPos = l.itemp.Pos
   380→}
   381→
   382→// Lex creates a new scanner for the input string.
   383→func Lex(input string) *Lexer {
   384→	l := &Lexer{
   385→		input: input,
   386→		state: lexStatements,
   387→	}
   388→	return l
   389→}
   390→
   391→// lineComment is the character that starts a line comment.
   392→const lineComment = "#"
   393→
   394→// lexStatements is the top-level state for lexing.
   395→func lexStatements(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   396→	if l.histogramState != histogramStateNone {
   397→		return lexHistogram
   398→	}
   399→	if l.braceOpen {
   400→		return lexInsideBraces
   401→	}
   402→	if strings.HasPrefix(l.input[l.pos:], lineComment) {
   403→		return lexLineComment
   404→	}
   405→
   406→	switch r := l.next(); {
   407→	case r == eof:
   408→		switch {
   409→		case l.parenDepth != 0:
   410→			return l.errorf("unclosed left parenthesis")
   411→		case l.bracketOpen:
   412→			return l.errorf("unclosed left bracket")
   413→		}
   414→		l.emit(EOF)
   415→		return nil
   416→	case r == ',':
   417→		l.emit(COMMA)
   418→	case isSpace(r):
   419→		return lexSpace
   420→	case r == '*':
   421→		l.emit(MUL)
   422→	case r == '/':
   423→		l.emit(DIV)
   424→	case r == '%':
   425→		l.emit(MOD)
   426→	case r == '+':
   427→		l.emit(ADD)
   428→	case r == '-':
   429→		l.emit(SUB)
   430→	case r == '^':
   431→		l.emit(POW)
   432→	case r == '=':
   433→		switch t := l.peek(); t {
   434→		case '=':
   435→			l.next()
   436→			l.emit(EQLC)
   437→		case '~':
   438→			return l.errorf("unexpected character after '=': %q", t)
   439→		default:
   440→			l.emit(EQL)
   441→		}
   442→	case r == '!':
   443→		if t := l.next(); t != '=' {
   444→			return l.errorf("unexpected character after '!': %q", t)
   445→		}
   446→		l.emit(NEQ)
   447→	case r == '<':
   448→		if t := l.peek(); t == '=' {
   449→			l.next()
   450→			l.emit(LTE)
   451→		} else if t := l.peek(); t == '/' {
   452→			l.next()
   453→			l.emit(TRIM_UPPER)
   454→		} else {
   455→			l.emit(LSS)
   456→		}
   457→	case r == '>':
   458→		if t := l.peek(); t == '=' {
   459→			l.next()
   460→			l.emit(GTE)
   461→		} else if t := l.peek(); t == '/' {
   462→			l.next()
   463→			l.emit(TRIM_LOWER)
   464→		} else {
   465→			l.emit(GTR)
   466→		}
   467→	case isDigit(r) || (r == '.' && isDigit(l.peek())):
   468→		l.backup()
   469→		return lexNumberOrDuration
   470→	case r == '"' || r == '\'':
   471→		l.stringOpen = r
   472→		return lexString
   473→	case r == '`':
   474→		l.stringOpen = r
   475→		return lexRawString
   476→	case isAlpha(r) || r == ':':
   477→		if !l.bracketOpen {
   478→			l.backup()
   479→			return lexKeywordOrIdentifier
   480→		}
   481→		switch r {
   482→		case ':':
   483→			if l.gotColon {
   484→				return l.errorf("unexpected colon %q", r)
   485→			}
   486→			l.emit(COLON)
   487→			l.gotColon = true
   488→			return lexStatements
   489→		case 's', 'S', 'm', 'M':
   490→			if l.scanDurationKeyword() {
   491→				return lexStatements
   492→			}
   493→		}
   494→		return l.errorf("unexpected character: %q, expected %q", r, ':')
   495→	case r == '(':
   496→		l.emit(LEFT_PAREN)
   497→		l.parenDepth++
   498→		return lexStatements
   499→	case r == ')':
   500→		l.emit(RIGHT_PAREN)
   501→		l.parenDepth--
   502→		if l.parenDepth < 0 {
   503→			return l.errorf("unexpected right parenthesis %q", r)
   504→		}
   505→		return lexStatements
   506→	case r == '{':
   507→		l.emit(LEFT_BRACE)
   508→		l.braceOpen = true
   509→		return lexInsideBraces
   510→	case r == '[':
   511→		if l.bracketOpen {
   512→			return l.errorf("unexpected left bracket %q", r)
   513→		}
   514→		l.gotColon = false
   515→		l.emit(LEFT_BRACKET)
   516→		if isSpace(l.peek()) {
   517→			skipSpaces(l)
   518→		}
   519→		l.bracketOpen = true
   520→		return lexDurationExpr
   521→	case r == ']':
   522→		if !l.bracketOpen {
   523→			return l.errorf("unexpected right bracket %q", r)
   524→		}
   525→		l.emit(RIGHT_BRACKET)
   526→		l.bracketOpen = false
   527→	case r == '@':
   528→		l.emit(AT)
   529→	default:
   530→		return l.errorf("unexpected character: %q", r)
   531→	}
   532→	return lexStatements
   533→}
   534→
   535→func lexHistogram(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   536→	switch l.histogramState {
   537→	case histogramStateMul:
   538→		l.histogramState = histogramStateNone
   539→		l.next()
   540→		l.emit(TIMES)
   541→		return lexValueSequence
   542→	case histogramStateAdd:
   543→		l.histogramState = histogramStateNone
   544→		l.next()
   545→		l.emit(ADD)
   546→		return lexValueSequence
   547→	case histogramStateSub:
   548→		l.histogramState = histogramStateNone
   549→		l.next()
   550→		l.emit(SUB)
   551→		return lexValueSequence
   552→	}
   553→
   554→	if l.bracketOpen {
   555→		return lexBuckets
   556→	}
   557→	switch r := l.next(); {
   558→	case isSpace(r):
   559→		l.emit(SPACE)
   560→		return lexSpace
   561→	case isAlpha(r):
   562→		l.backup()
   563→		return lexHistogramDescriptor
   564→	case r == ':':
   565→		l.emit(COLON)
   566→		return lexHistogram
   567→	case r == '-':
   568→		l.emit(SUB)
   569→		return lexHistogram
   570→	case r == 'x':
   571→		l.emit(TIMES)
   572→		return lexNumber
   573→	case isDigit(r):
   574→		l.backup()
   575→		return lexNumber
   576→	case r == '[':
   577→		l.bracketOpen = true
   578→		l.gotColon = false
   579→		l.gotDuration = false
   580→		l.emit(LEFT_BRACKET)
   581→		return lexBuckets
   582→	case r == '}' && l.peek() == '}':
   583→		l.next()
   584→		l.emit(CLOSE_HIST)
   585→		switch l.peek() {
   586→		case 'x':
   587→			l.histogramState = histogramStateMul
   588→			return lexHistogram
   589→		case '+':
   590→			l.histogramState = histogramStateAdd
   591→			return lexHistogram
   592→		case '-':
   593→			l.histogramState = histogramStateSub
   594→			return lexHistogram
   595→		default:
   596→			l.histogramState = histogramStateNone
   597→			return lexValueSequence
   598→		}
   599→	default:
   600→		return l.errorf("histogram description incomplete unexpected: %q", r)
   601→	}
   602→}
   603→
   604→func lexHistogramDescriptor(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   605→Loop:
   606→	for {
   607→		switch r := l.next(); {
   608→		case isAlpha(r):
   609→			// absorb.
   610→		default:
   611→			l.backup()
   612→
   613→			word := l.input[l.start:l.pos]
   614→			if desc, ok := histogramDesc[strings.ToLower(word)]; ok {
   615→				if l.peek() == ':' {
   616→					l.emit(desc)
   617→					return lexHistogram
   618→				}
   619→				l.errorf("missing `:` for histogram descriptor")
   620→				break Loop
   621→			}
   622→			// Current word is Inf or NaN.
   623→			if desc, ok := key[strings.ToLower(word)]; ok {
   624→				if desc == NUMBER {
   625→					l.emit(desc)
   626→					return lexHistogram
   627→				}
   628→			}
   629→			if desc, ok := counterResetHints[strings.ToLower(word)]; ok {
   630→				l.emit(desc)
   631→				return lexHistogram
   632→			}
   633→
   634→			l.errorf("bad histogram descriptor found: %q", word)
   635→			break Loop
   636→		}
   637→	}
   638→	return lexStatements
   639→}
   640→
   641→func lexBuckets(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   642→	switch r := l.next(); {
   643→	case isSpace(r):
   644→		l.emit(SPACE)
   645→		return lexSpace
   646→	case r == '-':
   647→		l.emit(SUB)
   648→		return lexNumber
   649→	case isDigit(r):
   650→		l.backup()
   651→		return lexNumber
   652→	case r == ']':
   653→		l.bracketOpen = false
   654→		l.emit(RIGHT_BRACKET)
   655→		return lexHistogram
   656→	case isAlpha(r):
   657→		// Current word is Inf or NaN.
   658→		word := l.input[l.start:l.pos]
   659→		if desc, ok := key[strings.ToLower(word)]; ok {
   660→			if desc == NUMBER {
   661→				l.emit(desc)
   662→				return lexStatements
   663→			}
   664→		}
   665→		return lexBuckets
   666→	default:
   667→		return l.errorf("invalid character in buckets description: %q", r)
   668→	}
   669→}
   670→
   671→// lexInsideBraces scans the inside of a vector selector. Keywords are ignored and
   672→// scanned as identifiers.
   673→func lexInsideBraces(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   674→	if strings.HasPrefix(l.input[l.pos:], lineComment) {
   675→		return lexLineComment
   676→	}
   677→
   678→	switch r := l.next(); {
   679→	case r == eof:
   680→		return l.errorf("unexpected end of input inside braces")
   681→	case isSpace(r):
   682→		return lexSpace
   683→	case isAlpha(r):
   684→		l.backup()
   685→		return lexIdentifier
   686→	case r == ',':
   687→		l.emit(COMMA)
   688→	case r == '"' || r == '\'':
   689→		l.stringOpen = r
   690→		return lexString
   691→	case r == '`':
   692→		l.stringOpen = r
   693→		return lexRawString
   694→	case r == '=':
   695→		if l.next() == '~' {
   696→			l.emit(EQL_REGEX)
   697→			break
   698→		}
   699→		l.backup()
   700→		l.emit(EQL)
   701→	case r == '!':
   702→		switch nr := l.next(); nr {
   703→		case '~':
   704→			l.emit(NEQ_REGEX)
   705→		case '=':
   706→			l.emit(NEQ)
   707→		default:
   708→			return l.errorf("unexpected character after '!' inside braces: %q", nr)
   709→		}
   710→	case r == '{':
   711→		return l.errorf("unexpected left brace %q", r)
   712→	case r == '}':
   713→		l.emit(RIGHT_BRACE)
   714→		l.braceOpen = false
   715→
   716→		if l.seriesDesc {
   717→			return lexValueSequence
   718→		}
   719→		return lexStatements
   720→	default:
   721→		return l.errorf("unexpected character inside braces: %q", r)
   722→	}
   723→	return lexInsideBraces
   724→}
   725→
   726→// lexValueSequence scans a value sequence of a series description.
   727→func lexValueSequence(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   728→	if l.histogramState != histogramStateNone {
   729→		return lexHistogram
   730→	}
   731→	switch r := l.next(); {
   732→	case r == eof:
   733→		return lexStatements
   734→	case r == '{' && l.peek() == '{':
   735→		if l.histogramState != histogramStateNone {
   736→			return l.errorf("unexpected histogram opening {{")
   737→		}
   738→		l.histogramState = histogramStateOpen
   739→		l.next()
   740→		l.emit(OPEN_HIST)
   741→		return lexHistogram
   742→	case isSpace(r):
   743→		l.emit(SPACE)
   744→		lexSpace(l)
   745→	case r == '+':
   746→		l.emit(ADD)
   747→	case r == '-':
   748→		l.emit(SUB)
   749→	case r == 'x':
   750→		l.emit(TIMES)
   751→	case r == '_':
   752→		l.emit(BLANK)
   753→	case isDigit(r) || (r == '.' && isDigit(l.peek())):
   754→		l.backup()
   755→		lexNumber(l)
   756→	case isAlpha(r):
   757→		l.backup()
   758→		// We might lex invalid Items here but this will be caught by the parser.
   759→		return lexKeywordOrIdentifier
   760→	default:
   761→		return l.errorf("unexpected character in series sequence: %q", r)
   762→	}
   763→	return lexValueSequence
   764→}
   765→
   766→// lexEscape scans a string escape sequence. The initial escaping character (\)
   767→// has already been seen.
   768→//
   769→// NOTE: This function as well as the helper function digitVal() and associated
   770→// tests have been adapted from the corresponding functions in the "go/scanner"
   771→// package of the Go standard library to work for Prometheus-style strings.
   772→// None of the actual escaping/quoting logic was changed in this function - it
   773→// was only modified to integrate with our lexer.
   774→func lexEscape(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   775→	var n int
   776→	var base, maxVal uint32
   777→
   778→	ch := l.next()
   779→	switch ch {
   780→	case 'a', 'b', 'f', 'n', 'r', 't', 'v', '\\', l.stringOpen:
   781→		return lexString
   782→	case '0', '1', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6', '7':
   783→		n, base, maxVal = 3, 8, 255
   784→	case 'x':
   785→		ch = l.next()
   786→		n, base, maxVal = 2, 16, 255
   787→	case 'u':
   788→		ch = l.next()
   789→		n, base, maxVal = 4, 16, unicode.MaxRune
   790→	case 'U':
   791→		ch = l.next()
   792→		n, base, maxVal = 8, 16, unicode.MaxRune
   793→	case eof:
   794→		l.errorf("escape sequence not terminated")
   795→		return lexString
   796→	default:
   797→		l.errorf("unknown escape sequence %#U", ch)
   798→		return lexString
   799→	}
   800→
   801→	var x uint32
   802→	for n > 0 {
   803→		d := uint32(digitVal(ch))
   804→		if d >= base {
   805→			if ch == eof {
   806→				l.errorf("escape sequence not terminated")
   807→				return lexString
   808→			}
   809→			l.errorf("illegal character %#U in escape sequence", ch)
   810→			return lexString
   811→		}
   812→		x = x*base + d
   813→		n--
   814→
   815→		// Don't seek after last rune.
   816→		if n > 0 {
   817→			ch = l.next()
   818→		}
   819→	}
   820→
   821→	if x > maxVal || 0xD800 <= x && x < 0xE000 {
   822→		l.errorf("escape sequence is an invalid Unicode code point")
   823→	}
   824→	return lexString
   825→}
   826→
   827→// digitVal returns the digit value of a rune or 16 in case the rune does not
   828→// represent a valid digit.
   829→func digitVal(ch rune) int {
   830→	switch {
   831→	case '0' <= ch && ch <= '9':
   832→		return int(ch - '0')
   833→	case 'a' <= ch && ch <= 'f':
   834→		return int(ch - 'a' + 10)
   835→	case 'A' <= ch && ch <= 'F':
   836→		return int(ch - 'A' + 10)
   837→	}
   838→	return 16 // Larger than any legal digit val.
   839→}

   841→// skipSpaces skips the spaces until a non-space is encountered.

   844→		l.next()

   846→	l.ignore()

   849→// lexString scans a quoted string. The initial quote has already been seen.
   850→func lexString(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   851→Loop:

   853→		switch l.next() {
   854→		case '\\':
   855→			return lexEscape

   857→			l.errorf("invalid UTF-8 rune")
   858→			return lexString
   859→		case eof, '\n':
   860→			return l.errorf("unterminated quoted string")
   861→		case l.stringOpen:
   862→			break Loop
   863→		}
   864→	}
   865→	l.emit(STRING)
   866→	return lexStatements
   867→}
   868→
   869→// lexRawString scans a raw quoted string. The initial quote has already been seen.
   870→func lexRawString(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   871→Loop:
   872→	for {
   873→		switch l.next() {
   874→		case utf8.RuneError:
   875→			l.errorf("invalid UTF-8 rune")
   876→			return lexRawString
   877→		case eof:
   878→			l.errorf("unterminated raw string")
   879→			return lexRawString
   880→		case l.stringOpen:
   881→			break Loop
   882→		}
   883→	}
   884→	l.emit(STRING)

   886→}
   887→
   888→// lexSpace scans a run of space characters. One space has already been seen.
   889→func lexSpace(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   890→	for isSpace(l.peek()) {
   891→		l.next()
   892→	}
   893→	l.ignore()

   898→func lexLineComment(l *Lexer) stateFn {

   901→		r = l.next()
   902→	}

   908→// lexNumber scans a number: decimal, hex, oct or float.
   909→func lexNumber(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   910→	if !l.scanNumber() {
   911→		return l.errorf("bad number syntax: %q", l.input[l.start:l.pos])
   912→	}
   913→	l.emit(NUMBER)
   914→	return lexStatements

   917→func (l *Lexer) scanDurationKeyword() bool {
   918→	for {
   919→		switch r := l.next(); {
   920→		case isAlpha(r):
   921→			// absorb.
   922→		default:
   923→			l.backup()
   924→			word := l.input[l.start:l.pos]
   925→			kw := strings.ToLower(word)
   926→			switch kw {
   927→			case "step":
   928→				l.emit(STEP)
   929→				return true
   930→			case "range":
   931→				l.emit(RANGE)
   932→				return true
   933→			case "min":
   934→				l.emit(MIN)
   935→				return true
   936→			case "max":
   937→				l.emit(MAX)
   938→				return true
   939→			default:
   940→				return false
   941→			}

   946→// lexNumberOrDuration scans a number or a duration Item.
   947→func lexNumberOrDuration(l *Lexer) stateFn {
   948→	if l.scanNumber() {
   949→		l.emit(NUMBER)

   951→	}

   953→	if acceptRemainingDuration(l) {
   954→		l.backup()
   955→		l.emit(DURATION)

   957→	}

   961→func acceptRemainingDuration(l *Lexer) bool {

   963→	if !l.accept("smhdwy") {
   964→		return false
   965→	}

   967→	// parse the duration.
   968→	l.accept("s")
   969→	// Next char can be another number then a unit.
   970→	for l.accept("0123456789") {
   971→		for l.accept("0123456789") {
   972→		}
   973→		// y is no longer in the list as it should always come first in
   974→		// durations.
   975→		if !l.accept("smhdw") {
   976→			return false
   977→		}
   978→		// Support for ms. Bad units like hs, ys will be caught when we actually
   979→		// parse the duration.
   980→		l.accept("s")
   981→	}
   982→	return !isAlphaNumeric(l.next())

   985→// scanNumber scans numbers of different formats. The scanned Item is
   986→// not necessarily a valid number. This case is caught by the parser.
   987→func (l *Lexer) scanNumber() bool {
   988→	initialPos := l.pos
   989→	// Modify the digit pattern if the number is hexadecimal.
   990→	digitPattern := "0123456789"
   991→	// Disallow hexadecimal in series descriptions as the syntax is ambiguous.
   992→	if !l.seriesDesc &&
   993→		l.accept("0") && l.accept("xX") {
   994→		l.accept("_") // eg., 0X_1FFFP-16 == 0.1249847412109375

   996→	}
   997→	const (
   998→		// Define dot, exponent, and underscore patterns.
   999→		dotPattern        = "."
  1000→		exponentPattern   = "eE"
  1001→		underscorePattern = "_"
  1002→		// Anti-patterns are rune sets that cannot follow their respective rune.
  1003→		dotAntiPattern        = "_."
  1004→		exponentAntiPattern   = "._eE" // and EOL.
  1005→		underscoreAntiPattern = "._eE" // and EOL.
  1006→	)
  1007→	// All numbers follow the prefix: [.][d][d._eE]*
  1008→	l.accept(dotPattern)
  1009→	l.accept(digitPattern)
  1010→	// [d._eE]* hereon.
  1011→	dotConsumed := false
  1012→	exponentConsumed := false
  1013→	for l.is(digitPattern + dotPattern + underscorePattern + exponentPattern) {
  1014→		// "." cannot repeat.
  1015→		if l.is(dotPattern) {
  1016→			if dotConsumed {
  1017→				l.accept(dotPattern)
  1018→				return false
  1019→			}
  1020→		}
  1021→		// "eE" cannot repeat.
  1022→		if l.is(exponentPattern) {
  1023→			if exponentConsumed {
  1024→				l.accept(exponentPattern)
  1025→				return false
  1026→			}
  1027→		}
  1028→		// Handle dots.
  1029→		if l.accept(dotPattern) {
  1030→			dotConsumed = true
  1031→			if l.accept(dotAntiPattern) {
  1032→				return false
  1033→			}
  1034→			// Fractional hexadecimal literals are not allowed.
  1035→			if len(digitPattern) > 10 /* 0x[\da-fA-F].[\d]+p[\d] */ {
  1036→				return false
  1037→			}
  1038→			continue
  1039→		}
  1040→		// Handle exponents.
  1041→		if l.accept(exponentPattern) {
  1042→			exponentConsumed = true
  1043→			l.accept("+-")
  1044→			if l.accept(exponentAntiPattern) || l.peek() == eof {
  1045→				return false
  1046→			}
  1047→			continue
  1048→		}
  1049→		// Handle underscores.
  1050→		if l.accept(underscorePattern) {
  1051→			if l.accept(underscoreAntiPattern) || l.peek() == eof {
  1052→				return false
  1053→			}
  1054→
  1055→			continue
  1056→		}
  1057→		// Handle digits at the end since we already consumed before this loop.
  1058→		l.acceptRun(digitPattern)
  1059→	}
  1060→	// Empty string is not a valid number.
  1061→	if l.pos == initialPos {
  1062→		return false
  1063→	}
  1064→	// Next thing must not be alphanumeric unless it's the times token
  1065→	// for series repetitions.
  1066→	if r := l.peek(); (l.seriesDesc && r == 'x') || !isAlphaNumeric(r) {
  1067→		return true
  1068→	}
  1069→	return false
  1070→}

  1072→// lexIdentifier scans an alphanumeric identifier. The next character

  1076→		// absorb

  1083→// lexKeywordOrIdentifier scans an alphanumeric identifier which may contain
  1084→// a colon rune. If the identifier is a keyword the respective keyword Item
  1085→// is scanned.
  1086→func lexKeywordOrIdentifier(l *Lexer) stateFn {
  1087→Loop:
  1088→	for {
  1089→		switch r := l.next(); {
  1090→		case isAlphaNumeric(r) || r == ':':
  1091→			// absorb.
  1092→		default:
  1093→			l.backup()
  1094→			word := l.input[l.start:l.pos]
  1095→			switch kw, ok := key[strings.ToLower(word)]; {
  1096→			case ok:
  1097→				// For fill/fill_left/fill_right, only treat as keyword if followed by '('
  1098→				// This allows using these as metric names (e.g., "fill + fill").
  1099→				// This could be done for other keywords as well, but for the new fill
  1100→				// modifiers this is especially important so we don't break any existing
  1101→				// queries.
  1102→				if kw == FILL || kw == FILL_LEFT || kw == FILL_RIGHT {
  1103→					if !l.peekFollowedByLeftParen() {
  1104→						l.emit(IDENTIFIER)
  1105→						break Loop
  1106→					}
  1107→				}
  1108→				l.emit(kw)
  1109→			case !strings.Contains(word, ":"):
  1110→				l.emit(IDENTIFIER)
  1111→			default:
  1112→				l.emit(METRIC_IDENTIFIER)
  1113→			}
  1114→			break Loop
  1115→		}
  1116→	}

  1118→		return lexValueSequence
  1119→	}

  1121→}

  1123→// peekFollowedByLeftParen checks if the next non-whitespace character is '('.
  1124→// This is used for context-sensitive keywords like fill/fill_left/fill_right

  1129→		if int(pos) >= len(l.input) {

  1134→			return r == '('
  1135→		}

  1137→	}
  1138→}

  1140→func isSpace(r rune) bool {
  1141→	return r == ' ' || r == '\t' || r == '\n' || r == '\r'

  1145→func isEndOfLine(r rune) bool {

  1162→func isAlpha(r rune) bool {

  1166→// lexDurationExpr scans arithmetic expressions within brackets for duration expressions.
  1167→func lexDurationExpr(l *Lexer) stateFn {

  1169→	case r == eof:
  1170→		return l.errorf("unexpected end of input in duration expression")

  1172→		l.emit(RIGHT_BRACKET)
  1173→		l.bracketOpen = false
  1174→		l.gotColon = false

  1176→	case r == ':':
  1177→		l.emit(COLON)

  1179→			return l.errorf("unexpected colon before duration in duration expression")
  1180→		}
  1181→		if l.gotColon {
  1182→			return l.errorf("unexpected repeated colon in duration expression")
  1183→		}
  1184→		l.gotColon = true
  1185→		return lexDurationExpr
  1186→	case r == '(':
  1187→		l.emit(LEFT_PAREN)
  1188→		l.parenDepth++
  1189→		return lexDurationExpr
  1190→	case r == ')':
  1191→		l.emit(RIGHT_PAREN)
  1192→		l.parenDepth--
  1193→		if l.parenDepth < 0 {
  1194→			return l.errorf("unexpected right parenthesis %q", r)
  1195→		}
  1196→		return lexDurationExpr
  1197→	case isSpace(r):
  1198→		skipSpaces(l)
  1199→		return lexDurationExpr
  1200→	case r == '+':
  1201→		l.emit(ADD)
  1202→		return lexDurationExpr
  1203→	case r == '-':
  1204→		l.emit(SUB)
  1205→		return lexDurationExpr
  1206→	case r == '*':
  1207→		l.emit(MUL)
  1208→		return lexDurationExpr
  1209→	case r == '/':
  1210→		l.emit(DIV)
  1211→		return lexDurationExpr
  1212→	case r == '%':
  1213→		l.emit(MOD)
  1214→		return lexDurationExpr
  1215→	case r == '^':
  1216→		l.emit(POW)
  1217→		return lexDurationExpr
  1218→	case r == ',':
  1219→		l.emit(COMMA)
  1220→		return lexDurationExpr
  1221→	case r == 's' || r == 'S' || r == 'm' || r == 'M' || r == 'r' || r == 'R':
  1222→		if l.scanDurationKeyword() {
  1223→			return lexDurationExpr
  1224→		}

  1227→		l.backup()

  1232→	}
  1233→}
  1234→
  1235→// findPrevRightParen finds the previous right parenthesis.
  1236→// Use in case when the parser had to read ahead to the find the next right
  1237→// parenthesis to decide whether to continue and lost track of the previous right
  1238→// parenthesis position.
  1239→// Only use when outside string literals as those can have runes made up of
  1240→// multiple bytes, which would break the position calculation.
  1241→// Falls back to the input start position on any problem.
  1242→// https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/16053
  1243→func (l *Lexer) findPrevRightParen(fallbackPos posrange.Pos) posrange.Pos {
  1244→	// Early return on:
  1245→	// - invalid fallback position,
  1246→	// - not enough space for second right parenthesis,
  1247→	// - last read position is after the end, since then we stopped due to the
  1248→	//   end of the input, not a parenthesis, or if last position doesn't hold
  1249→	//   right parenthesis,
  1250→	// - last position doesn't hold right parenthesis.
  1251→	if fallbackPos <= 0 || fallbackPos > posrange.Pos(len(l.input)) || l.lastPos <= 0 || l.lastPos >= posrange.Pos(len(l.input)) || l.input[l.lastPos] != ')' {
  1252→		return fallbackPos
  1253→	}
  1254→	for i := l.lastPos - 1; i > 0; i-- {
  1255→		switch {
  1256→		case l.input[i] == ')':
  1257→			return i + 1
  1258→		case isSpace(rune(l.input[i])):
  1259→		default:
  1260→			return fallbackPos
  1261→		}
  1262→	}
  1263→	return fallbackPos
  1264→}

(\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"unicode\"\n\t\"unicode/utf8\"\n\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser/posrange\"\n)\n\n// Item represents a token or text string returned from the scanner.\ntype Item struct {\n\tTyp ItemType     // The type of this Item.\n\tPos posrange.Pos // The starting position, in bytes, of this Item in the input string.\n\tVal string       // The value of this Item.\n}\n\n// String returns a descriptive string for the Item.\nfunc (i Item) String() string {\n\tswitch {\n\tcase i.Typ == EOF:\n\t\treturn \"EOF\"\n\tcase i.Typ == ERROR:\n\t\treturn i.Val\n\tcase i.Typ == IDENTIFIER || i.Typ == METRIC_IDENTIFIER:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%q\", i.Val)\n\tcase i.Typ.IsKeyword():\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<%s>\", i.Val)\n\tcase i.Typ.IsOperator():\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<op:%s>\", i.Val)\n\tcase i.Typ.IsAggregator():\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<aggr:%s>\", i.Val)\n\tcase len(i.Val) > 10:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%.10q...\", i.Val)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%q\", i.Val)\n}\n\n// Pretty returns the prettified form of an item.\n// This is same as the item's stringified format.\nfunc (i Item) Pretty(int) string { return i.String() }\n\n// IsOperator returns true if the Item corresponds to a arithmetic or set operator.\n// Returns false otherwise.\nfunc (i ItemType) IsOperator() bool { return i > operatorsStart && i < operatorsEnd }\n\n// IsAggregator returns true if the Item belongs to the aggregator functions.\n// Returns false otherwise.\nfunc (i ItemType) IsAggregator() bool { return i > aggregatorsStart && i < aggregatorsEnd }\n\n// IsAggregatorWithParam returns true if the Item is an aggregator that takes a parameter.\n// Returns false otherwise.\nfunc (i ItemType) IsAggregatorWithParam() bool {\n\treturn i == TOPK || i == BOTTOMK || i == COUNT_VALUES || i == QUANTILE || i == LIMITK || i == LIMIT_RATIO\n}\n\n// IsExperimentalAggregator defines the experimental
